top10.introHtml: Top 10 picks for a cheap, fun second car near Media, PA — ranked by condition and value over raw mileage. All are within 35 miles of ZIP 19063.

picks.whyGood: E46 325Ci is the sweet spot of the BMW convertible lineup — balanced chassis, bulletproof inline-six, and dead-simple mechanically vs. later BMWs. 60k miles is genuinely low for a 2001. Great Deal rating, only 15 miles away at a dealer who stocks multiple classics. Manual or auto, both are rewarding top-down.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate soft top through full open/close cycle. Check headliner and rear carpet for water staining from top seal. Look for oil leaks at valve cover and oil filter housing gasket (E46 trait). Check subframe mounting point for cracks (pre-2001 issue but inspect anyway). Test all window regulators.
picks.bottomLine: The right BMW at the right price — classic drop-top less than 20 minutes from Media.

picks.whyGood: Only 39k miles on a 2008 Solstice is exceptional — this car has barely been driven. The Solstice's Kappa platform is pure sports car: short wheelbase, rear-wheel drive, manual available. Great Deal rating. Clean title + no accidents (Cars.com filter applied). 30 miles is worth the drive for these numbers.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate soft top — inspect rear plastic window for cracks and haze. Check for oil consumption (Ecotec 2.4L can consume oil if not serviced). Inspect rocker panels for rust bubbling. Test clutch feel if manual. Look for water staining in footwells.
picks.bottomLine: 39k miles on a Solstice is nearly impossible to find — grab it before someone else does.

picks.whyGood: 32k miles on a 2006 Solstice is incredible — this is essentially a lightly used car. Rear-wheel drive, available manual, proper sports car proportions. Clean title and no accidents confirmed. Good Deal rating. 28 miles away with a photo showing it in good shape.
picks.whatToVerify: Same Solstice checks: full top operation, rear window condition, rocker panel rust, oil consumption, clutch/transmission. Being a 2006, check for any early-build quality issues (rattles, window regulator clips).
picks.bottomLine: 32k miles on a fun sports car at $13k — rare at this price point.

picks.whyGood: Under 30k miles on a 2010 MINI Cooper S convertible is remarkable. The S trim adds the turbo engine and significantly more fun. MINI convertibles are legitimately entertaining in traffic and tight roads. Clean title + no accidents filter applied. Fair Deal rating may mean room to negotiate.
picks.whatToVerify: MINI timing chain tensioner recall (check if done — R57 Cooper S has known issue). Inspect soft top seams and rear window. Check for oil leaks from valve cover. Look for coolant hose condition. Test AC — R134a systems on this gen often need recharge.
picks.bottomLine: Nearly new mileage on a go-kart with a sunroof — the S makes all the difference.

picks.whyGood: A 2013 BMW 1-Series convertible at $9,500 is a genuine deal — this is a modern BMW with electric soft top, great stereo, and the 3.0L N52 inline-six. 69k miles is modest. Great Deal rating. The 1-Series coupe/convertible is the last true rear-wheel-drive 1er and drives brilliantly.
picks.whatToVerify: Check electric top operation: close fully, listen for motor strain. Inspect seals around top perimeter. N52 engine: check for VANOS rattle on cold start, coolant expansion tank condition (plastic cracks). Check for oil leaks. Test all iDrive functions.
picks.bottomLine: $9,500 for a modern BMW convertible with a real inline-six — the value math works.

picks.whyGood: The GXP trim means turbocharged 260hp — a completely different animal from the base Solstice. 51k miles, 25 miles away. This is a genuinely quick sports car that embarrasses many more expensive convertibles. Available with 5-speed manual.
picks.whatToVerify: Turbo inlet hose (common crack point on LE5T). Check intercooler for leaks. Inspect top operation and rear window condition. Clutch feel and throw on manual. Look for oil consumption — turbos accelerate this if PCV isn't maintained.
picks.bottomLine: Turbocharged Solstice GXP for under $12k is a sleeper value play.

picks.whyGood: The Z3 is the quintessential fun weekend convertible — lightweight, rear-wheel drive, great steering. The 2.3L M52 is one of BMW's best naturally-aspirated engines. 75k miles is quite reasonable. Good Deal rating at a real dealer 22 miles away. Clean title + no accident history confirmed.
picks.whatToVerify: Z3 soft tops are body-color painted on the exterior flap — inspect for cracking. Check rear window defroster. Look for rust on rocker sills and behind rear wheels. M52 coolant system: expansion tank and lower radiator hose. Diff fluid if not recently changed.
picks.bottomLine: Classic BMW roadster at a real-world price — exactly what this search is for.

picks.whyGood: The Saturn Sky shares the Solstice's Kappa platform but has a more refined interior and slightly different styling. 39k miles is exceptional. Great Deal rating. Clean title + no accidents. The Sky tends to be a bit cheaper than the Solstice for the same mechanicals.
picks.whatToVerify: Same as Solstice: soft top operation, rear window condition, rocker rust, oil consumption, clutch. Check for battery drain issues (Sky had some electrical gremlins). Verify top latches properly and seals fully.
picks.bottomLine: Same fun as the Solstice, great deal rating, barely used — strong value pick.

picks.whyGood: The B6 A4 Cabriolet with the 3.0L V6 quattro is a genuinely elegant top-down car. Quattro all-wheel drive means year-round usability. 80k miles is reasonable for the age and platform. Great Deal rating. Clean title + no accidents. Langhorne is 34 miles — worth the trip.
picks.whatToVerify: Audi B6 convertible top: operate fully and check hydraulic fluid reservoir (behind rear seat). Inspect for oil leaks at timing chain cover. Check headliner attachment points. Quattro driveshaft boots. Listen for timing chain rattle on cold start.
picks.bottomLine: V6 quattro Audi cabrio under $9k — refined, AWD, and genuinely rare at this price.

picks.whyGood: 177k miles sounds high but the NC Miata (2006–2015) routinely hits 250k+ with basic maintenance. The Miata is the gold standard of affordable fun convertibles: light, balanced, manual available, bulletproof engine. Grand Touring trim adds leather and Bose. 20 miles away at a local dealer.
picks.whatToVerify: Miata NC: check for rust on rocker panels and around the top mounting points. Verify timing chain hasn't been replaced recently (check records — NC doesn't have the timing belt issues of older Miatas). Inspect soft top for tears. Look for coolant leaks at water pump. 177k is fine IF oil changes were regular — ask for service records.
picks.bottomLine: The Miata benchmark — high miles but these engines don't quit if maintained.

mustangs.introHtml: Ford Mustang convertibles within budget. SN-95 (1994–2004) and S197 (2005–2014) generations within 35 miles of Media, PA.

picks.whyGood: SN-95 Mustang GT with only 55k miles is a find. The 5.0 GT40 V8 produces 215hp and sounds fantastic with the top down. 25 miles away. SN-95 GT convertibles are gaining collector interest and this mileage is half what you typically see.
picks.whatToVerify: SN-95 convertible: inspect the top-mount-to-rocker reinforcement for rust (common failure point). Check for frame rust at rear subframe. T-45 transmission third-gear synchro (shift slowly into 3rd). Look for rear axle seal leaks. Soft top operation and rear window condition. Oil leaks at rear main seal.
picks.bottomLine: Low-mile SN-95 GT V8 — the grunt and sound are the point, and 55k barely breaks in a Mustang.

picks.whyGood: 44k miles on a 1994 Mustang is genuinely remarkable. Only 28 miles away. If this is a GT or Cobra, it's a serious collector piece. Even as a V6 base, 44k miles means the car has been babied. Clean title + no accidents per Cars.com filter.
picks.whatToVerify: Confirm trim level (GT vs. V6 base — huge difference in value and fun). SN-95 rust inspection same as above. Check for airbag module condition. Verify convertible top motor operates smoothly. With 44k miles, verify it hasn't sat for years (dry-rotted seals, old fuel).
picks.bottomLine: 44k miles on a 31-year-old Mustang — whatever the trim, this one has been cared for.

picks.whyGood: S197 Mustang V6 Premium at $10,499 with a Great Deal rating. The 2011+ 3.7L V6 produces 305hp — nearly as much as the old GT V8 — and gets much better fuel economy. 64k miles is modest. Clean title + no accidents. 30 miles.
picks.whatToVerify: S197 convertible: check rear seat area for water intrusion from top seal. Test IRS rear suspension if equipped (listen for clunk on takeoff). T6060 manual if present: 3rd gear synchro test. Check for electronic throttle body codes. Inspect top latching mechanism.
picks.bottomLine: 305hp for $10.5k with a Great Deal badge — the modern V6 Premium is more car than its reputation suggests.
